observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate Officer to undertake a collaborative effort to trace the noted variances of Land account amounting to P 4,620,055 and subsequently effect corrections/adjusting entries thereof.
Direct the management, more particularly the two offices involved, to employ systematic procedures such as but not limited to dedicating personnel from both offices specifically for reconciliation of records alone at least once every year.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the IC to provide the cost/valuation of the donated and patented land properties of the LGU or otherwise, constitute an Appraisal Committee that will do the valuation;
Require the Municipal Accountant to appropriately record the properties vis-à-vis the documents that support and/or prove the LGU’s ownership therewith; and
Ensure that the MACCO and the GSO to coordinate with each other and effect a periodic reconciliation of PPE accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Henceforth direct the IC which is the headed by the OIC- GSO, to completely mobilize the Committee to prepare a PIP prior to the conduct of physical count of PPE and submit the same to the COA at least 10 days before the scheduled start of the inventory and devote themselves exclusively, as required by the guidelines, to the conduct of physical count until completed.
To ensure the Committee’s familiarity with the  COA Circular No. 2020-006 to heed the general and procedural guidelines on inventory taking of the PPE accounts to ensure that faithful compliance therewith is enforced.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the MTO and the MACCO to coordinate with each other and regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with that appearing per books of accounts of the LGU monthly or at least quarterly for the timely adjustment of discrepancies and variances to ensure accuracy of the reported Cash in Bank account balances in the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
To direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a written letter request, signed by the Municipal Mayor to the AGDBs for the monthly Bank Statement to facilitate immediate action from the bank and to be used as a tracer for subsequent follow-up if not acted upon immediately by the bank; and
Ensure compliance with the monthly preparation of the BRS and subsequent submission of the same to the COA along with the paid check and original copies of debit/credit memos within 10 days after the receipt of the Bank Statements.

Recommendations:
Henceforth, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor:
To direct the MDRRMC through the MDRRMO to further enhance its procedures in preparing the MDRRMFIP including the identification of PPAs that will cover the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management, and the proper review and counter-checking before the finalization and approval of such; and
To ensure that all information required in the COA Circular are incorporated in the MDRRMFIP to maintain transparency and accountability and also for reconciliation of related reports and records.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to coordinate with each other for the monthly preparation of the Report of Sources and Utilization of the MDRRMF and its submission on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002. And simultaneously, submit the report to the Regional Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (RDRRMC) through the Regional Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnish the Regional Offices of the DILG and DBM as required  in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

Recommendations:
Henceforth, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Ensure that the Municipality strictly complies with the prescribed guidelines before granting any special allowance to its personnel; and
Lead the management to establish the regularity of the grant to the personnel noted by submitting a detailed accomplishment report and daily time record of each personnel to validate payment thereof.

Recommendation:
Henceforth, we recommend to the Municipal Mayor to direct the BAC to ensure the submission of copies of the perfected contracts of the Municipality together with all the supporting documents as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 within five (5) working days from execution to enable the initial auditorial and technical review of the contract.

Recommendations:
We recommended management through the General Service Office to ensure the preparation of a complete Driver’s Trip Ticket, as well as the preparation and submission to COA of the Monthly Report of Official travels and the Monthly Fuel Consumption of all government vehicles for purposes of evaluating the reasonableness of fuel consumption.
Also, we recommended management minimize if not eliminate the fuelling of private vehicles out of government sources for official purposes by instituting strict controls and monitoring measures. Likewise, allowing the grant of fuel for the private vehicles of officers/employees receiving Transportation Allowance is recommended to be stopped as this is tantamount to double compensation which is illegal and disallowable in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to continue lead the management in all efforts to maximize the utilization of the fund and ensure the achievement of the optimum implementation of projects that will ultimately promote and contribute to the inclusive economic growth and development of the LGU.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the following
officials to do the following courses of actions pursuant to COA Circular
2015-008, to wit:

    A. Municipal Accountant

        1. Maintain separate  subsidiary  ledgers  for  the  road  and  its
           components namely: a) road lot, b) road  pavement,  c)  drainage
           and slope protection  structures,  and  d)  other  miscellaneous
           structures.


        2. Reconcile the subsidiary ledger of the road and  its  components
           with  the  Local  Road  Inventory  and  Road   Map   to   ensure
           completeness.

    B. Municipal Engineer


        1. Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the  local
           roads with identified cost;


        2. Determine the  components  of  local  roads  identified  in  the
           inventory without corresponding cost and recognize the  cost  of
           each component at its fair value;


        3. Provide the local accountant and the  general  services  officer
           with the complete  description  and  cost  segregation  of  road
           components for road projects.


    C.  Municipal General Services Officer


        1. Maintain a Local Road Inventory and  Road  Map  in  coordination
           with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer;


        2. Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for  all  roads
           and its components.




    D. Inventory Committee


        1. Conduct physical count and prepare the Report  on  the  Physical
           Count of the Road Network system.

observations.recommendation: The Accountable Officers should initiate the immediate liquidation of all the cash advances for travels granted to him/her or refund the amount which remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2015 to preclude the issuance of Notice of Disallowance and avoid inclusion in the filing of malversation charge as required by the COA unnumbered Memorandum dated February 05, 2014. That the provisions of COA Circular no. 97–002 dated February 10, 1997, specifically on the liquidation of all cash advances should be strictly observed. The Municipal Accountant should discontinue in granting of additional cash advances for travels if the previous cash advance is not yet first settled or a proper accounting thereof was made. He must likewise be cautious in observing strictly the provisions of Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445. Regular monitoring of unliquidated cash advances should also be done and call the attention of personnel/employees concerned by issuing demand letters to concerned Accountable Officers.

We recommend that all funds and/or cash balances of the Municipality with the One Network Bank, Padada branch shall have to be immediately transferred to any of the GFIs doing business in Digos City, at the choice of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality’s management shall establish a well-defined functional structure on procurement service which, among others, delineates responsibility for the submission of Purchase Orders to the audit team.

We recommend to reconsidering the CSC MC No. 6, Series 2012 and CSC Resolution No 120048, promulgated March 12, 2012. Memorandum Circular No. 6, series of 2012, thus, depriving the Municipality to have an objective tool to assess its own and its personnel’s performance.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Act be revisited, implemented and enforced with more vigor and tenacity so as to protect and promote the right to health of the Municipality’s constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Municipality’s BAC to submit copies of duly served written invitations to a national association of contractors duly recognized by the CIAP and/or relevant chamber of commerce and industry and to a non-government organization pertinent to those consummated procurement. In the event none were made, written justifications may be submitted. Henceforth, written invitations shall be served to a national association of contractors duly recognized by the CIAP and to a non-government organization pertinent to future procurement of infrastructure projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Accountant and all Department Heads to adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for supplies and materials provided under Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual. In addition, we also recommend that actual on-site inspection and/or physical inventory be conducted by the Municipality’s authorized committee of three to determine if there still existed substantial unused supplies which committee’s report may be used a basis in adjusting the expense and inventory accounts and/or recognizing the unused or unconsumed supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor cease the practice of granting allowances to the concerned government employee to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures and issuance of Notice of Disallowance from the audit team. Furthermore, it is advised that the Municipality observe strictly the provisions under Section 4.5 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007 – 1 dated April 23, 2007 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 003 dated October 29, 2012. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should stop the payment of allowances to the concerned government employee. She must be guided with the provisions cited; otherwise, all the persons responsible for the expenditures shall be jointly and severally liable for the incurrence of irregular disbursements.
The designated accountant shall require the immediate liquidation or proper accounting.
